sentences.text: Task 1. You are going to listen to five texts.
For each of them, answer the two questions given.
Mark the correct answer A, B, or C.
You have 20 seconds to look through the task.
You will hear the recording twice.
Text 1. I remember two funny things, which happened to me when my father first took me to Thbilisi.
I was six then.
I remember two funny things, which happened to me when my father first took me to Thbilisi.
At the station my father stopped a taxi and asked the driver to take us to Africa.
When I heard this, I started to cry and told my father that I wanted to go to Thbilisi and not to Africa.
My father laughed and said that we were in Thbilisi, but my uncle, with whom we were going to stay, lived in a part of Thbilisi called Africa.
The next day, my father took me to Turtle Lake.
I became quite sad when we arrived there.
I couldn't understand why this place was called Turtle Lake, as there were no turtles there.
Then, I thought maybe it was because, the people were walking there very slowly, like turtles.
When we got back home to Oni, I told everybody that, two funny things had happened to me in Thbilisi.
One was that I lived in Africa, and the other was that I saw Turtle Lake.
I remember two funny things, which happened to me, when my father first took me to Thbilisi.
I was six then.
At the station my father stopped a taxi and asked the driver to take us to Africa.
When I heard this, I started to cry and told my father that I wanted to go to Thbilisi and not to Africa.
My father laughed and said that we were in Thbilisi, but my uncle, with whom we were going to stay, lived in a part of Thbilisi called Africa.
The next day, my father took me to Turtle Lake.
I became quite sad, when we arrived there.
I couldn't understand, why this place was called Turtle Lake, as there were no turtles there.
Then, I thought maybe it was because, the people were walking there very slowly, like turtles.
When we got back home to Oni, I told everybody that, two funny things had happened to me in Thbilisi.
One was that I lived in Africa, and the other was that I saw Turtle Lake.

Gabriel García Márquez is a well-known Colombian writer, the winner of the Nobel Prize for literature.
Márquez was raised by his grandparents who had a big influence on his future career, as a writer.
In his novels and short stories Márquez made great use of the wonderful stories, real and imaginary, that his grandparents told him.
His grandfather, who was an officer in the army during the Civil War in Colombia, was an excellent storyteller,
Civil War and its heroes. His grandmother's stories were very different. She told him
unbelievable, magical stories full of unreal, imaginary elements. Marquez enjoyed his grandmother's
unique way of telling stories. This influenced Marquez's style of writing and this is especially
well seen in his most popular, award-winning novel 100 Years of Solitude. Gabriel Garcia Marquez is a

in Paris, which you probably do not know. For example, do you know?
That the Eiffel Tower was designed as a temporary building, planned to be pulled down after 20 years?
And, do you know, that the Eiffel Tower initially was not at all popular?
When Gustave Eiffel's company built this monument for the 1889 World Fair,
many people didn't like the massive iron structure. The campaign against the Eiffel Tower was led by
some of the most prominent Parisians of the time. One of them was a famous French writer,
Guy de Maupassant.
Who often had lunch in the restaurant of the Eiffel Tower. As Guy de Maupassant used to say,
it was the only place in Paris, where he didn't have to look at the building he hated.
These protesters were on the wrong side of history as today, the Eiffel Tower is one of
the most recognizable monuments in the world, attracting more visitors than any other paid
tourist site. There are two things about the Eiffel Tower in Paris, which you probably do not know.

Ilya Kosukishvili and Nino Ramishvili, the soloists of the Georgian Opera and Ballet Theatre,
were invited to London to participate in the International Folk Dance Festival.
The festival was held under the patronage of Lady Elizabeth Beausleon, the mother of the British
Queen. But Nino Ramishvili was not allowed to go to the UK as Soviet authorities thought that,
being from the bourgeois family, she could stay there forever. And this was in spite of the fact
that they knew very well that Nino Ramishvili was a British queen. Nino Ramishvili was a British queen,
Nino Ramashvili was an excellent dancer. So, Iliko Sukashvili performed in London alone and won the
prestigious Grand Prix of the festival. Inspired by impressions of the festival and the award he
received there, in 1945, Iliko Sukashvili together with his wife Nino Ramashvili founded the Georgian
State Dance Company. The company, which was the first professional state dance company in Georgia,
was later renamed to Sukashvili Georgian National Ballet. In 1935, during the Soviet times,

The body was from the time of the Roman invasion of Celtic Britain.
Despite being 2000 years old and unlike other discoveries of this age, this body had skin, hair and internal organs.
The scientists who examined the body were able to look inside the man's stomach and find the food that he'd eaten for his last meal.
The man was so well preserved because he was in a watery environment, safe from the bacteria that need oxygen to live.
The water was of special quality. It was acid water and this preserved the body for a long time.

Hello. My full name is Shakira Isabel Mebarak Ripol, but the whole world knows me as Shakira.
I come from Colombia. Today, I'm a successful singer, but there was a time when my career
almost came to an end. It happened in late 2017. I suffered from a problem in my throat,
and I could hardly speak. I always thought that at some point in my life,
some things would go away, like attractiveness, youth, all of that stuff.
But I never thought that my voice would lead me, because it's so fundamental for my personality.
So, it was extremely difficult for me when I couldn't sing.
There were times I couldn't even get out of bed. I was so depressed.
There is a joke that a man often wishes to have married a dumb wife, or a wife who cannot speak.
But when I had to remain quiet, my partner, Gerard Piquet, felt like one of those ex-prisoners
who was given his freedom to sing.
And doesn't know what to do with it.
I was very upset and pessimistic.
I have never fought with my partner so much as in those times when I was unable to speak.
I had to communicate through signs, and nobody could understand me.
My sons, two and four then, couldn't read, so writing didn't help.
When my voice eventually returned, without an operation, I was extremely happy.
I was glad that I had not agreed to surgery.
I tried meditation, even going to Lourdes, a major religious site for Catholic believers to get holy water.
I don't know exactly what helped me, but I was ready for the stage.
My sixth tour, called the El Dorado Tour, had been postponed due to my throat problems.
But when I finally went on that tour, it was very different.
I was out there because I wanted to feel the pleasure of singing.
Before, I was a singer.
Before, I was a singer.
Before, I was a singer.
Before, I was a singer.
Before, I was a singer.
Before, I was a singer.
I would usually go to the stage and sing to convince myself that I'm good and that people like me.
But the El Dorado Tour, every night on stage, was a gift.
I think this tragic experience has made me a better singer.
I had to overcome so many difficulties to become an international pop singer.
Today, I live in Barcelona with Gerard and our two sons.
When I first went on a tour after my sons were born, I had no idea how this was going to work.
When I first went on a tour after my sons were born, I had no idea how this was going to work.
At some point, I thought it was going to be impossible.
It is true that being separated from my sons was hard.
My kids were so little.
I missed even their bad behavior.
I tried to arrange the dates so that they would coincide with school holidays and they could be with me.
But other times, they stayed at home with Gerard.
Being a mother is the hardest job I've ever done.
It could affect your creativity unless you protect your personality.
When I'm with my children, I try to tell them about problems their dad and I had to overcome.
But I never overload them with every detail of my career or every victory.
They weren't born when I was back in Colombia and every single door was shut in my face.
Those are the stories I want to tell them because life isn't always easy.
Not everything happens as you've planned.
When my children attended my show for the first time,
I said,
I'm going to do it.
I'm going to do it.
I'm going to do it.
I'm going to do it.
I'm going to do it.
I'm going to do it.
I'm going to do it.
I saw that they looked a little confused.
I'm trying to give them a normal life, but it is not always possible.
There is huge social inequality in Colombia.
So I initiated a foundation to help schools and children in Colombia.
After I became popular, I knew I had to succeed and make money.
Because only that way, I could do something for my country.
